anti_patterns.description: A low-tier source cites a high-tier source, but the claim it makes is not actually present in the cited source. The intermediary borrows authority it hasn't earned.
anti_patterns.signature: Follow citation links and verify the cited source actually supports the derived claim. Mismatch between intermediary claim and cited source content.
anti_patterns.remediation: Score the claim based on the intermediary's tier, not the cited source's tier.

anti_patterns.description: Multiple sources making the same claim, but all tracing back to a single original source. Appears to be corroboration but is actually amplification.
anti_patterns.signature: Track syndication chains. Deduplicate sources by origin and original publication date. Multiple sources with shared origin characteristics making identical novel claims.
anti_patterns.remediation: Count as one source for corroboration purposes.

anti_patterns.description: Presenting outdated information as current, or mixing claims from different time periods without noting the discrepancy.
anti_patterns.signature: Compare claim timestamps with valid_until fields and flag mismatches.
anti_patterns.remediation: Require temporal context on all claims with bounded validity.

anti_patterns.description: Presenting vague or estimated information with false precision. E.g. 'The project will cost $4,237,891' when the source says 'approximately $4.2 million.'
anti_patterns.signature: Compare extracted numerical claims against source excerpts for precision inflation.
anti_patterns.remediation: Preserve the precision level of the original source.

anti_patterns.description: Presenting absolute numbers without the base rate or comparison that makes them meaningful. '50 incidents reported' without noting whether that's out of 100 or 100,000.
anti_patterns.signature: Flag numerical claims that lack a reference frame (percentage, per-capita, year-over-year, compared to what).
anti_patterns.remediation: Attach contextualizing evidence that provides the denominator.

anti_patterns.description: Only finding sources that support a claim because contradicting sources are behind paywalls, taken offline, or not indexed.
anti_patterns.signature: Track source retrieval failures (404s, paywalls, robot blocks) and flag claims where potential counter-evidence was inaccessible.
anti_patterns.remediation: Note accessibility gaps in the evidence record. Lower confidence when significant counter-evidence sources were unreachable.

anti_patterns.description: Demanding trust without showing evidence. 'This claim is verified' without a visible provenance chain. Any claim presented without accessible evidence links has failed the most basic defense.
anti_patterns.signature: Audit snapshot chunks for claims without embedded evidence references.
anti_patterns.remediation: Block snapshot deployment if any chunk contains a 'verified' or 'corroborated' claim without at least one evidence link.

anti_patterns.description: Claiming the system is objective or unbiased rather than transparent. Objectivity is an assertion that invites attack. Transparency is a practice that resists it.
anti_patterns.signature: Review system-generated descriptions and documentation for claims of neutrality or objectivity.
anti_patterns.remediation: Replace claims of neutrality with descriptions of process: 'Loom evaluates evidence using a published hierarchy' not 'Loom provides unbiased information.'

anti_patterns.description: Quietly fixing errors instead of publishing corrections. Silent fixes undermine the public changelog and make 'you quietly changed it' a valid attack.
anti_patterns.signature: Diff snapshots for claim changes that lack corresponding changelog entries.
anti_patterns.remediation: Block snapshot deployment if claims changed between versions without changelog entries.

anti_patterns.description: Making the challenge process difficult to discourage dissent. High barriers to challenge protect the system from flooding but also protect it from legitimate correction.
anti_patterns.signature: Monitor challenge submission rates, auto-close rates, and time-to-resolution. A sudden drop in challenges may indicate suppression, not satisfaction.
anti_patterns.remediation: Publish challenge statistics. If auto-close rates exceed 80% for 30 days, review the triage criteria for excessive strictness.

anti_patterns.description: Allowing individual curators unchecked power over claims. A single curator who can resolve contradictions, reclassify sources, and restructure concept maps without review has too much power.
anti_patterns.signature: Monitor curator activity concentration. Flag when a single curator accounts for >30% of resolutions in a 30-day window.
anti_patterns.remediation: Require second-curator review when concentration thresholds are exceeded.
